text: Sustained attention — the capacity to hold focus on a single task over extended time — depends on a circuit linking the prefrontal cortex to the locus coeruleus, a small brainstem nucleus that releases norepinephrine. As norepinephrine tone declines during prolonged work, the signal-to-noise ratio in prefrontal neurons drops, and mind-wandering increases. This is not a character flaw; it is neurochemical physics. The brain's arousal system cannot maintain peak gain indefinitely. Deliberate rest — even a two-minute pause — partially restores norepinephrine tone, buying another window of clean, directed focus before the next inevitable drift.
question: According to the passage, what happens in the prefrontal cortex when norepinephrine tone declines?

options.text: The signal-to-noise ratio drops, leading to increased mind-wandering.
The locus coeruleus becomes overactive and floods the cortex with signal.
Dopamine compensates by binding to prefrontal receptors.
Sustained attention improves as the brain shifts to a default mode.
